title: Woman charged in fatal South Boston stabbing
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Gisselle Pascual, 24, charged with manslaughter in South Boston stabbing, police report.
Incident occurred at 258 Old Colony Ave; victim died in hospital after stabbing.
Pascual's charges upgraded from assault to manslaughter; arraignment set in South Boston District Court.

title: Brockton police shoot man who allegedly fired at officers, man sleeping nearby also injured, DA says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: A 44-year-old man allegedly fired at Brockton police officers during a pursuit and was shot in the shoulder.
A 71-year-old man was grazed by a bullet while sleeping in a nearby home during the gunfire exchange.
Both men were hospitalized with non-life-threatening injuries, and state police are investigating the incident.

title: Former Bruins coach Bruce Cassidy abruptly fired by Vegas before playoffs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruce Cassidy was fired by the Vegas Golden Knights less than a month before the Stanley Cup Playoffs.
John Tortorella replaces Cassidy as head coach, with Vegas currently third in the Pacific Division at 36-26-16.
Vegas Golden Knights struggled recently with poor goalie performance, contributing to six losses in their last seven games.

title: New NFL mock draft has Patriots addressing major need on defense with 31st pick
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: NFL.com’s Mike Band predicts Patriots will pick Clemson's T.J. Parker with 31st pick in NFL Draft.
T.J. Parker, a Clemson DE, recorded 126 tackles and 21.5 sacks in 39 games over three seasons.
Patriots need pass-rushing help after losing K'Lavon Chaisson; added Dre'Mont Jones in free agency.

title: North Shore police department asking for public’s help after theft of historical plaques
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Newburyport police are investigating multiple thefts of historical bronze plaques, including one from the African American section of Old Hill graveyard.
The plaque, stolen on March 24, commemorates Black individuals buried in the 18th and 19th centuries in Newburyport.
Authorities request community assistance in reporting information related to the thefts, urging residents to call 978-462-4411 with tips.

title: Bruins sign Merrimack goalie Max Lundgren to entry-level contract
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins signed Merrimack College goalie Max Lundgren to a one-year, entry-level contract for the 2026-27 season.
Lundgren posted a 21-16-2 record with a 2.55 goals-against average and .920 save percentage in 39 games last season.
Lundgren, from Angelholm, Sweden, was named MVP of the Hockey East tournament, aiding Merrimack in their first title win.

title: 2 people from Mass. killed when helicopter crashes in Hawaii
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Massachusetts residents Margaret Rimmler, 65, and Patrick Haskell, 59, died in a helicopter crash in Kauai, Hawaii.
The helicopter, operated by Airborne Aviation, crashed on a sandbar near Kalalau Beach with five people onboard.
Two survivors were medically evacuated to a Lihue medical center; one additional victim's identity is pending family notification.

title: Review & setlist: Diana Krall brought a special kind of magic to The Cabot
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: Diana Krall performed at The Cabot in Beverly on March 28, 2026, showcasing her piano and vocal talents.
Accompanied by bassist John Clayton, Krall's performance included covers of jazz standards and original compositions.
The setlist featured songs like 'Almost Like Being In Love,' 'I Just Found Out About Love,' and 'P.S. I Love You.'

title: Chelsea teen charged with murder after allegedly chasing down, stabbing man
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Sergio Josue Castellanos Cerrato, 18, charged with murder for allegedly stabbing Eduardo Alfredo Rosales Lopez in Chelsea.
Lopez was chased and attacked by a group including Castellanos Cerrato near Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue.
Police found blood-stained clothing and weapons linked to Castellanos Cerrato; he's held without bail, due in court April 21.

title: 2025 marked Boston area’s slowest year of post-pandemic population growth, data shows
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston metro's population growth was lowest since 2021, rising by only 11,991 people from 2024 to 2025.
Statewide, Massachusetts saw a growth of 15,524 in 2025, its smallest increase since post-pandemic recovery began.
Middlesex and Worcester counties led in growth, yet increases were significantly lower compared to previous years.
